title: SIMPLE MUTTON CURRY RECIPE FOR BEGINNERS | QUICK AND EASY MUTTON CURRY
description: Simple Mutton Curry Recipe | Quick & Easy Mutton Curry Recipe | Mutton Curry Recipe for Beginners | Mutton Curry Recipe for Bachelors | Mutton Gravy | Indian Mutton Curry Recipe | Simple and Easy Mutton Curry | How to make Mutton Curry Ingredients: - Mutton with bones- 500 - 600 gms - Onions chopped- 3 medium
ingredients: - Mutton with bones- 500 - 600 gms | - Onions chopped- 3 medium | - Ginger garlic paste- 2 tsp | - Tomatoes- 2 medium chopped | - Green cardamom- 4 | - Cloves- 5 | - Cinnamon- 3 pieces | - Black cardamom- 2 | - Peppercorns- 10 | - Whole dry red chillies- 2 | - Turmeric powder- 1/2 tsp | - Red Chilli powder- 2 tsp | - Coriander powder- 2 tsp | - Cumin powder- 1 tsp | - Whisked curd/yogurt- 3 tbsp | - Salt- 1 tsp | - Oil- 3 tbsp
instructions: - Heat oil in a pan. | - Add all the whole garam masalas and stir. | - Now add the dry red chillies and mix well. | - Add the chopped onions and fry for around 10 mins on medium heat till light brown in colour. | - Now add the ginger garlic paste and fry for around 2 mins till the raw smell is gone. | - Put in the Mutton pieces, the powders and 1 tsp salt. | - Now fry on high for around 2 mins and then bhuno on medium for around 10-12 mins till the Mutton pieces are browned and oil separates. | - Switch off flame/ lower heat and add in the whisked curd/yogurt. | - Mix on low for 2-3 mins and then on medium for around 5 mins till the curd is cooked and oil separates. | - Now add in the chopped tomatoes and cook for around 10 mins till soft. | - Add 2 cups of water, mix & cook for 2 mins. | - Now transfer this to a pressure cooker and cook till 3-4 whistles. | Allow to cool before opening the pressure cooker. | - Alternatively, if the mutton is tender you can continue to cook in the pan till the meat is soft. | - Serve hot with roti or rice.
raw_text: Simple Mutton Curry Recipe | Quick & Easy Mutton Curry Recipe | Mutton Curry Recipe for Beginners | Mutton Curry Recipe for Bachelors | Mutton Gravy | Indian Mutton Curry Recipe | Simple and Easy Mutton Curry | How to make Mutton Curry Ingredients: - Mutton with bones- 500 - 600 gms - Onions chopped- 3 medium - Ginger garlic paste- 2 tsp - Tomatoes- 2 medium chopped - Green cardamom- 4 - Cloves- 5 - Cinnamon- 3 pieces - Black cardamom- 2 - Peppercorns- 10 - Whole dry red chillies- 2 - Turmeric powder- 1/2 tsp - Red Chilli powder- 2 tsp - Coriander powder- 2 tsp - Cumin powder- 1 tsp - Whisked curd/yogurt- 3 tbsp - Salt- 1 tsp - Oil- 3 tbsp Preparation: Chop the onions and the tomatoes. Process: - Heat oil in a pan. - Add all the whole garam masalas and stir. - Now add the dry red chillies and mix well. - Add the chopped onions and fry for around 10 mins on medium heat till light brown in colour. - Now add the ginger garlic paste and fry for around 2 mins till the raw smell is gone. - Put in the Mutton pieces, the powders and 1 tsp salt. Mix well. - Now fry on high for around 2 mins and then bhuno on medium for around 10-12 mins till the Mutton pieces are browned and oil separates. - Switch off flame/ lower heat and add in the whisked curd/yogurt. - Mix on low for 2-3 mins and then on medium for around 5 mins till the curd is cooked and oil separates. - Now add in the chopped tomatoes and cook for around 10 mins till soft. - Add 2 cups of water, mix & cook for 2 mins. - Now transfer this to a pressure cooker and cook till 3-4 whistles. Allow to cool before opening the pressure cooker. - Alternatively, if the mutton is tender you can continue to cook in the pan till the meat is soft. - Serve hot with roti or rice. #simplemuttoncurry #easymuttoncurry #muttoncurryrecipe #indianmuttoncurry #spiceeatsmutton #spiceeatsrecipes #bachelormuttoncurry #muttonmasala #muttongravy Simple Mutton Curry For Bachelors and Beginners | जब जानेंगे इतनी आसान Mutton Curry का राज़ तो बनाएंगे घर पर आज | Indian Mutton Curry Beginners Recipe | Mutton Curry Recipe | Mutton Curry for Beginners | Easy Recipe for Bachelors | Simple Mutton Curry | Easy Mutton Curry for Beginners | Quick and Easy Mutton Curry | Indian Mutton Curry for Beginners | Indian Mutton Curry Gravy | Super Easy Mutton Curry | Easy Mutton Recipe | Mutton Curry Music: https://www.bensound.com/royalty-free-music

http://bit.ly/RBAppAndroid 📲𝗶𝗢𝗦 - http://bit.ly/RBAppiOS *********************** DHABA MEAT RECIPE Preparation time 10 minutes Cooking time 30-35 minutes Serve 2-4 Ingredients 2-3 tbsp Oil, तेल 2 tbsp Ghee, घी 2 Black cardamom, बड़ी इलायची 2-4 Cloves, लॉन्ग 8-10 Black peppercorns, काली मिर्च के दाने Prepared Ginger Garlic paste, अदरक लहसुन का पेस्ट 5 medium size Onion, chopped, प्याज Salt to taste, नमक स्वादअनुसार 1 ½ tbsp Coriander powder, धनिया पाउडर Marinated Mutton, मैरिनेटेड मटन 2-3 cups Water, पानी Prepared Onion Tomato Masala, तैयार किया हुआ प्याज टमाटर मसाला For Ginger Garlic Paste ½ inch Ginger, peeled, slice, अदरक 5-6 Garlic cloves, लहसुन 2 Green chillies, हरी मिर्च For Mutton Marination 800 gms Mutton, with bones, मटन 1 ½ tsp Degi red chilli powder, देगी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 tsp Turmeric powder, हल्दी पाउडर Salt to taste, नमक स्वादअनुसार ½ cup Curd, beaten, दही For Onion Tomato Masala 1 tbsp Oil, तेल ½ tsp Cumin seeds, जीरा 1 tsp Coriander seeds, धनिया के बीज 2 Bay leaf, तेजपत्ता 1 large Onion, chopped, प्याज 2 Green chillies, chopped, हरी मिर्च ½ tbsp Coriander powder, धनिया पाउडर 1 tsp Degi red chilli powder, देगी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 2 small Tomatoes, chopped, टमाटर Salt to taste, नमक स्वादअनुसार Little water, पानी 1 tbsp tender Coriander stems, finely chopped, धनिया के डंठल For Garnish 1 tsp Dry fenugreek leaves, crushed, कसूरी मेथी ½ inch Ginger, julienned, अदरक Coriander sprig, धनिया पत्ता Process In a cooker, add oil, ghee once it's hot, add black cardamom, cloves, black peppercorns and let it splutter well. Add ginger garlic paste, onion and saute it well for 5 minutes. Add salt to taste and saute it until light brown in color. Add coriander powder and mix well. Add marinated mutton and mix it well. Add water and mix well. Cover it with a lid and cook on medium flame for 5-6 whistles. Let it cool at room temperature until pressure inside the cooker goes down naturally. Open the lid, add prepared onion tomato masala and mix it well. Just cover it with the lid without a whistle and cook on medium flame for 5-7 minutes . Transfer it into a serving bowl, garnish it with dry fenugreek leaves, ginger, coriander sprig. Serve hot with roti or steam rice. For Ginger Garlic Paste In a mortar pestle, add ginger, garlic, green chillies and grind it into a rough paste and keep aside for further use. For Mutton Marination In a large bowl, add mutton, degi red chilli powder, turmeric powder, salt to taste, curd and marinated well. Keep it for 10-15 minutes. For Onion Tomato Masala In a shallow pan, add oil, once it's hot, add cumin seeds, coriander seeds, bay leaf and let it splutter well. Add onion, green chillies and saute it well, add coriander powder, degi red chilli powder mix it well. Add tomatoes, salt to taste and saute well. add little water and let it cook on medium flame for 2-4 minutes. Add tender coriander stems and mix it well. *********************** For more fantastic recipes, check out the Ranveer Brar App: 📲𝐀𝐧𝐝𝐫𝐨𝐢𝐝 - http://bit.ly/RBAppAndroid 📲𝗶𝗢𝗦 - http://bit.ly/RBAppiOS *********************** 🌎 Follow Ranveer Brar on your favorite Social Media channels: ➡️ https://www.facebook.com/RanveerBrar/ ➡️ https://www.instagram.com/ranveer.brar/ ➡️ https://twitter.com/ranveerbrar #DhabaMuttoncurry #dhabameatrecipe #ranveerbrar #Dhabamutton #कुकरढाबामटनकरी #मटनकरी #spicymutton #muttoncurry #muttonrecipe #muttonbiryani #Dhabagosht #cookermuttoncurry #muttonkulambu #dhabastylemutton #militaryhotelmuttoncurry #muttoncurryranveerbrar

ingredients: - Mutton, curry cut with bones- 500 gms | - Onions sliced- 2 medium | - Ginger garlic paste- 2 tsp | - Green cardamom- 4 | - Cloves- 4 | - Cinnamon- 3 pieces | - Black cardamom- 2 | - Bay leaf- 1 | Spice powders to be mixed in 1/2 cup of water | - Turmeric powder- 1/2 tsp | - Red Chilli powder- 2 tsp | - Coriander powder- 2 tsp | - Cumin powder- 1 tsp | - Whisked curd/yoghurt- 3 tbsp | - Kashmiri Chilli powder- 1 tsp | - Garam masala powder- 1/2 tsp | - Salt to taste - 1 tsp | - Oil- 4 tbsp | - Mix the spice powders in 1/2 cup of water.

raw_text: Mutton Curry | Easy Mutton Curry in Pressure Cooker | Tasty Mutton Curry in Pressure Cooker | Quick Mutton Curry in Pressure Cooker | Mutton Curry Recipe | How to Make Mutton Curry | Mutton Curry in Pressure Cooker Ingredients for Mutton Curry: - Mutton, curry cut with bones- 500 gms - Onions sliced- 2 medium - Ginger garlic paste- 2 tsp Whole spices: - Green cardamom- 4 - Cloves- 4 - Cinnamon- 3 pieces - Black cardamom- 2 - Bay leaf- 1 Spice powders to be mixed in 1/2 cup of water - Turmeric powder- 1/2 tsp - Red Chilli powder- 2 tsp - Coriander powder- 2 tsp - Cumin powder- 1 tsp - Whisked curd/yoghurt- 3 tbsp - Kashmiri Chilli powder- 1 tsp - Garam masala powder- 1/2 tsp - Salt to taste - 1 tsp - Oil- 4 tbsp Preparation: - Slice the onions. - Mix the spice powders in 1/2 cup of water. Process: - Heat oil in a pressure cooker. - Add the whole spices and bay leaf, give a stir. - Add the sliced onions and fry on medium heat for around 8-10 mins till golden. - Add the ginger garlic paste, mix and fry on low heat for around 2 mins on low heat. - Pour in the masala mix and fry on low heat for around 2-3 mins till oil separates. - Now add the mutton pieces and fry on high heat for around 2-3 mins mixing it well. - Add salt and mix. - Continue to bhunno/fry on medium heat for around 7-8 mins till the spices coat the mutton. - Now add the whisked curd/yogurt on low heat and mix it well. Continue to mix it on low heat for 3-4 mins till it is cooked and oil separates. - Add in the Kashmiri Chilli powder, mix and cook for 1-2 mins on low heat. - Now add 1.5 cups of water, mix well and close the pressure cooker. - Pressure cook for 4 whistles. - OR - If using an electric pressure cooker or instant pot cook for 12-15 minutes - OR - Continue to cook covered in the pan on low heat for around 45 mins till mutton is tender. Add more water if required. - Once done, add the garam masala powder and mix well. Simmer for 2 mins and add chopped coriander leaves as garnish. - Serve hot with roti or rice. #muttoncurry #pressurecookermuttoncurry #easymuttoncurry #muttoncurryrecipe #tastymuttoncurry #simplemuttoncurry #spiceeats #spiceeatsrecipes #spiceeatsmutton Music: https://www.bensound.com/royalty-free-music

instructions: Add the mutton into a large bowl along with salt, turmeric powder & the remaining ingredients of the marinade, mix well using your hands, you can marinate the mutton for a few hours if you have the time. | Set a pressure cooker over high flame & add the oil & ghee into it, once the ghee gets hot, add in all the whole spices along with the onions, stir well & cook over high flame until the onions turn golden brown. | Once the onions turn light golden brown, add in the ginger garlic chilli paste & slit green chillies, stir well & continue to cook until the onions turn golden brown. | Further lower the flame & add all the powdered spices, stir & immediately add a splash of hot water & cook the spices for 2-3 minutes until the fat gets separated. | Once the ghee gets separated, add the marinated mutton & salt, stir well & sear the mutton over high flame for 4-5 minutes. | Then add hot water as required to cover the mutton & mix well, put the lid on & cook the mutton over high flame until the first whistle & then set the flame to low & cook for 12-15 minutes, the time taken for the mutton to be cooked will depend upon its quality. | Once cooked, switch off the flame & let the cooker de-pressurize, open the lid after the cooker has de-pressurized & if the mutton hasn’t cooked fully, you can cook it over high flame for a few more minutes until it is done. | Further, switch on the flame to high & stir it well, then add garam masala, roasted kasuri methi powder, lemon juice & fresh coriander, stir & cook for 1-2 minutes. | Your easy mutton curry is ready, serve it hot with rotis & rice. | Garam masala - https://youtu.be/xih_pk8O6DU
raw_text: Full written recipe for Mutton Curry (cooker) Prep time: 10-15 minutes Cooking time: 25-30 minutes Serves: 3-4 people Ingredients: Marination MUTTON | मटन 1 KG SALT | नमक TO TASTE TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP LEMON JUICE | निंबू का रस 1 TSP GINGER GARLIC CHILLI PASTE | अदरक लेहसुन और मिर्च की पेस्ट 3 TBSP CURD | दही 200 GRAMS Curry OIL | तेल 2-3 TBSP GHEE | घी 2-3 TBSP WHOLE SPICES CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलायची 2 NOS. BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 2 NOS. BLACK CARDAMOM | बड़ी इलाइची 1 NO. CINNAMON STICK | दालचीनी 1 INCH ONION | प्याज़ 350 GRAMS (SLICE) GINGER GARLIC CHILLI PASTE | अदरक लेहसुन और मिर्च की पेस्ट 2 TBSP GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (SLIT) POWDERED SPICES TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 2 TBSP SPICY RED CHILLI | तीखी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TBSP GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला 1/2 TSP HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH SALT | नमक TO TASTE HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | भुनी हुई कसूरी मेथी का पाउडर A PINCH LEMON JUICE | निंबू का रस 1 TSP FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A LARGE HANDFUL Method: Add the mutton into a large bowl along with salt, turmeric powder & the remaining ingredients of the marinade, mix well using your hands, you can marinate the mutton for a few hours if you have the time. Set a pressure cooker over high flame & add the oil & ghee into it, once the ghee gets hot, add in all the whole spices along with the onions, stir well & cook over high flame until the onions turn golden brown. Once the onions turn light golden brown, add in the ginger garlic chilli paste & slit green chillies, stir well & continue to cook until the onions turn golden brown. Further lower the flame & add all the powdered spices, stir & immediately add a splash of hot water & cook the spices for 2-3 minutes until the fat gets separated. Once the ghee gets separated, add the marinated mutton & salt, stir well & sear the mutton over high flame for 4-5 minutes. Then add hot water as required to cover the mutton & mix well, put the lid on & cook the mutton over high flame until the first whistle & then set the flame to low & cook for 12-15 minutes, the time taken for the mutton to be cooked will depend upon its quality. Once cooked, switch off the flame & let the cooker de-pressurize, open the lid after the cooker has de-pressurized & if the mutton hasn’t cooked fully, you can cook it over high flame for a few more minutes until it is done. Further, switch on the flame to high & stir it well, then add garam masala, roasted kasuri methi powder, lemon juice & fresh coriander, stir & cook for 1-2 minutes. Your easy mutton curry is ready, serve it hot with rotis & rice. ingredients: - Goat meat - 1 kg (with fat and bones) | - Ginger garlic paste- 3 tsp | - Whisked Curd- 200 gms | - Fried onions made into a paste- 4 tbsp | - (around 3 medium onions fried & made into a paste) | - Turmeric powder- 1/2 tsp | - Red Chilli powder- 2 tsp | - Coriander powder- 4 tsp | - Mace/ Javitri powder- 1/2 tsp | - Nutmeg/ Jaiphal powder- 1/2 tsp | - Green cardamom - 6 | - Black cardamom- 2 | - Cinnamon- 4 small pieces | - Cloves- 6 | - Black peppercorns- 10-12 | - Salt - 1 tsp | - Oil- 4 tbsp | - Kewra water- 1/4 tsp | - Slice 3 medium onions, fry them in oil till golden brown in colour. instructions: - Take a pressure cooker or pressure pan and heat 4 tbsp of oil. | - Now add the whole garam masala & once fragrant add the mutton pieces. | - Mix well & keep frying on high for around 7-8 mins till mutton changes colour and leaves water. | - Now add ginger garlic paste, mix & keep frying on high to medium for around 10 mins. | - Add all the masalas/ powders - Red Chilli Powder, Turmeric powder, Coriander powder & salt. | - Mix well & “bhuno” the meat till oil separates. | - Now add enough water to cover the mutton & pressure cook for 3-4 whistles. | - Allow it to cool before opening the lid. | - Place the cooker back on heat & cook on high for around 2-3 mins to dry up any excess water. | - Now switch off the gas & add the whisked curd & mix it well. | - Put it back on flame & keep mixing & cooking for around 5 mins for the curd to get cooked. | - Now add the fried onion paste/ powder, mix it well & cook for around 10 mins till oil separates. | - Add the Javitri/Mace Powder & Jaiphal/ Nutmeg Powder, mix well & simmer for 2 mins. | - Lastly add the keora water, mix & simmer for 2 mins. | - Serve hot with roti or rice.
raw_text: Mutton Korma Delhi Style | Purani Dilli Ka Mutton Korma | Mutton Curry Delhi Style | Mutton Korma Restaurant Recipe | पुरानी दिल्ली का असली मटन कोरमा | Karim's Mutton Korma | Shahi Mutton Korma Ingredients: - Goat meat - 1 kg (with fat and bones) - Ginger garlic paste- 3 tsp - Whisked Curd- 200 gms - Fried onions made into a paste- 4 tbsp - (around 3 medium onions fried & made into a paste) - Turmeric powder- 1/2 tsp - Red Chilli powder- 2 tsp - Coriander powder- 4 tsp - Mace/ Javitri powder- 1/2 tsp - Nutmeg/ Jaiphal powder- 1/2 tsp Whole garam masala/ spices: - Green cardamom - 6 - Black cardamom- 2 - Cinnamon- 4 small pieces - Cloves- 6 - Black peppercorns- 10-12 - Salt - 1 tsp - Oil- 4 tbsp - Kewra water- 1/4 tsp Preparation: - Wash & clean the mutton pieces & drain the water. - Slice 3 medium onions, fry them in oil till golden brown in colour. Take it out & allow to cool. Crush them with hand till powdery. Take out 4 tbsp of that and keep aside. Process: - Take a pressure cooker or pressure pan and heat 4 tbsp of oil. - Now add the whole garam masala & once fragrant add the mutton pieces. - Mix well & keep frying on high for around 7-8 mins till mutton changes colour and leaves water. - Now add ginger garlic paste, mix & keep frying on high to medium for around 10 mins. - Add all the masalas/ powders - Red Chilli Powder, Turmeric powder, Coriander powder & salt. - Mix well & “bhuno” the meat till oil separates. - Now add enough water to cover the mutton & pressure cook for 3-4 whistles. - Allow it to cool before opening the lid. - Place the cooker back on heat & cook on high for around 2-3 mins to dry up any excess water. - Now switch off the gas & add the whisked curd & mix it well. - Put it back on flame & keep mixing & cooking for around 5 mins for the curd to get cooked. - Now add the fried onion paste/ powder, mix it well & cook for around 10 mins till oil separates. - Add the Javitri/Mace Powder & Jaiphal/ Nutmeg Powder, mix well & simmer for 2 mins. - Lastly add the keora water, mix & simmer for 2 mins. - Serve hot with roti or rice. #muttonkorma #muttonkormadelhistyle #muttoncurrydelhistyle #karimsmuttonkorma #muttonkormarecipe #spiceeatsmutton #spiceeats #spiceeatsrecipes #karimsmuttonkorma पुरानी दिल्ली का असली मटन कोरमा | Mutton Korma Restaurant Recipe | Mutton Korma| Delhi Style Mutton Korma | Restaurant Style | Mutton Korma Delhi Style | Purani Dilli Ka Mutton Korma | Mutton Curry Delhi Style | Mutton Korma Restaurant Recipe | पुरानी दिल्ली का असली मटन कोरमा Music: https://www.bensound.com/royalty-free-music

times until the mutton is fully cooked. | You can also cook the mutton in a pressure cooker at this stage but cooking it over low flame gives better results. | The mutton will release a lot of moisture so make sure that you don’t add any additional water, also make a point that you don’t keep opening the lid very frequently or else the steam will keep escaping & the mutton will take longer you can add hot water as required to adjust the consistency as per your liking. | Now heat a pan & add ghee into it, once the ghee heats up add the garam masala & kasuri methi to the ghee & stir it well. | Pour the ghee over the mutton & add fresh coriander & lemon juice, stir it really well & finally add a small handful of fresh coriander. | Your mutton curry is ready, serve it hot with tandoori rotis, rice bhaakri & rice.

BLACK CARDAMOM | बडी इलायची 10-12 nos. FENNEL SEEDS | सौंफ 1 TBSP MACE | जावित्री 2 NOS. STONE FLOWER | पत्थर के फूल 2 GRAMS FOX NUTS | मखाना 10-12 NOS. (GRATED) SALT | नमक A LARGE PINCH Marination Ingredients: FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया 3/4 CUP GARLIC | लेहसुन 30-35 CLOVES GREEN CHILLIES | हरी मिर्च 6-7 NOS. GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH DRY COCONUT | खोपरा 1/4 WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED MUTTON | मटन 2 KG SALT | नमक 1 TBSP TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP VAATAN | वाटन 1/2 QUANTITY CURD | दही 300 GRAMS (WHISKED) Cooking the curry Ingredients: OIL | तेल 6-7 TBSP WHOLE SPICES CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1.5 TSP GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलायची 4 NOS. BLACK CARDAMOM | बड़ी इलाइची 2 NOS. CINNAMON | दालचीनी 2 INCH BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 2 NOS. ONION | प्याज़ 700 GRAMS / 10-12 NOS. MEDIUM SIZED (CHOPPED) VATAN | वाटन REMAINING HALF QUANTITY POWDERED SPICES TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP SPICY RED CHILLI | तीखी लाल मिर्च 2 TBSP KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER| कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 2 TBSP CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 2 TBSP GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला 1 TBSP CUMIN POWDER | जीरा पाउडर 2 TBSP HOT WATER | गरम पानी 100-150 ML GHEE | घी 3-4 TBSP GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH KASURI METHI | कसूरी मेथी 1 TSP FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया AS REQUIRED (CHOPPED) LEMON JUICE | नींबू का रस 1 TSP Method: Set a handi on high flame & let it heat well, then add the oil into it & let the oil heat nicely as well. Further add all the whole spices & the chopped onions, stir well & cook over medium flame until the onions turn golden brown, keep stirring at regular intervals so that the onions don’t burn. Once the onions turn golden brown add the remaining half quantity of the prepared vaatan, stir well & cook the vaatan for 3-4 minutes. After cooking the vaatan for 3-4 minutes, lower the flame & add all the powdered spices, stir & immediately add hot water & cook until the oil separates. Once the oil separates, add the marinated mutton & stir it well, you’ll have to cook the mutton over high flame for 10-15 minutes, you’ll need to keep stirring the mutton at regular intervals. After cooking it for 10-15 minutes over high flame, cover the handi with a parat & pour water over it. Now lower the flame & continue to cook the mutton for an hour or until the mutton is completely cooked. Once the water over the parat heats up, discard it & replace it with fresh water, the water will take upto 15-20 minutes so heat up so you will have to repeat this process 2-3 times until the mutton is fully cooked. You can also cook the mutton in a pressure cooker at this stage but cooking it over low flame gives better results. The mutton will release a lot of moisture so make sure that you don’t add any additional water, also make a point that you don’t keep opening the lid very frequently or else the steam will keep escaping & the mutton will take longer time to cook. Once you have cooked the mutton completely, you’ll notice that a beautiful semi-thick gravy will have formed in the handi without addition of water, if you want the gravy to be thinner you can add hot water as required to adjust the consistency as per your liking. Now heat a pan & add ghee into it, once the ghee heats up add the garam masala & kasuri methi to the ghee & stir it well. Pour the ghee over the mutton & add fresh coriander & lemon juice, stir it really well & finally add a small handful of fresh coriander. title: Champaran Mutton | चंपारन मटन | Handi Mutton | Bihari Style Mutton Curry | Sanjeev Kapoor Khazana
description: Kick off your weekend with a burst of flavor with our 'Champaran Mutton' recipe, also known as Ahuna or Handi Meat. This rich and spicy dish from Bihar is perfect for a #FuntasticFriday, adding a delightful touch to your weekend feast! CHAMPARAN MUTTON Ingredients 750 grams mutton, cut into 2-inch pieces on the bone
ingredients: 750 grams mutton, cut into 2-inch pieces on the bone | 1½ tbsps coriander seeds | 1 tbsp cumin seeds | 18-20 black peppercorns | 10 cloves | 20-25 green cardamoms | 4-5 black cardamoms | ½ tbsp fennel seeds | 1 blade of mace | 2 pieces of stone flower | ¼ cup puffed lotus seeds | 6-7 dried red chillies, stemmed | 2 star anise | ½ tsp poppy seeds | Salt to taste | 1 cup mustard oil | 4 large onions, sliced | 3-4 green cardamoms | 3-4 cloves | 2 bay leaves | 1 inch cinnamon | 2 black cardamoms | 1 star anise | 2 tbsps Kashmiri red chilli powder | 1 tbsp coriander powder | 1 tsp cumin powder | ¼ tsp turmeric powder | 2 tbsps ginger-garlic paste | 2-3 green chillies, slit | 6-7 dried red chillies, stemmed | 2 whole garlic pods | Refined flour dough as required
instructions: Heat a nonstick pan. | Add coriander seeds and cumin seeds and dry roast for 30 seconds. | Add black peppercorns, cloves, green cardamoms, black cardamoms, fennel seeds, mace, stone flower, lotus seeds, dried red chillies and star anise and sauté for 3-4 minutes or till fragrant. | Add poppy seeds, nutmeg and sauté for 30 seconds. | Transfer on a plate and allow to cool. | Heat a small pan. | Add mustard oil and allow to smoke. | Set aside to cool down. | Transfer the roasted mixture in a grinder jar, add salt and grind to fine powder. | Take mutton in a bowl. | Add onions, green cardamoms, cloves, bay leaves, cinnamon, black cardamoms, star anise, Kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, turmeric powder, 2 tbsps prepared spice mix, ginger-garlic paste and mix well. | Add salt and ½ cup mustard oil and mix well. | Set aside to marinate for 6-8 hours. | Preheat the oven at 160⁰C. | Add green chillies, dried red chillies and garlic pods and mix well. | Add mustard oil in earthenware pot and add marinated mutton on top. | Drizzle remaining mustard oil on top. | Seal the pot with refined flour dough, make a hole on one side for the steam to escape and place in the preheated oven. | Cook for 1 hour 15 minutes. | Hold the pot with a cloth duster from both the sides and shake after every 15 minutes. | Seal the hole and set aside for 10-15 minutes. | Garnish with coriander sprig and serve hot with phulkas. | Click to Subscribe: http://bit.ly/1h0pGXf | For more recipes : http://www.sanjeevkapoor.com | Get Certified on Sanjeev Kapoor Academy : https://www.sanjeevkapooracademy.com/ | To get recipes on your Google or Alexa devices, click here : https://www.klovechef.ai/signup | Best cooked in Wonderchef Kitchenware. | Buy Now on : https://goo.gl/eB9kQq | Facebook : http://www.facebook.com/ChefSanjeevKapoor | Twitter : https://twitter.com/sanjeevkapoor | Instagram : https://www.instagram.com/sanjeevkapoor
raw_text: Kick off your weekend with a burst of flavor with our 'Champaran Mutton' recipe, also known as Ahuna or Handi Meat. This rich and spicy dish from Bihar is perfect for a #FuntasticFriday, adding a delightful touch to your weekend feast! CHAMPARAN MUTTON Ingredients 750 grams mutton, cut into 2-inch pieces on the bone Spice mix 1½ tbsps coriander seeds 1 tbsp cumin seeds 18-20 black peppercorns 10 cloves 20-25 green cardamoms 4-5 black cardamoms ½ tbsp fennel seeds 1 blade of mace 2 pieces of stone flower ¼ cup puffed lotus seeds 6-7 dried red chillies, stemmed 2 star anise ½ tsp poppy seeds A small piece of nutmeg Salt to taste Mutton 1 cup mustard oil 4 large onions, sliced 3-4 green cardamoms 3-4 cloves 2 bay leaves 1 inch cinnamon 2 black cardamoms 1 star anise 2 tbsps Kashmiri red chilli powder 1 tbsp coriander powder 1 tsp cumin powder ¼ tsp turmeric powder 2 tbsps ginger-garlic paste 2-3 green chillies, slit 6-7 dried red chillies, stemmed 2 whole garlic pods Refined flour dough as required Fresh coriander sprigs for garnish Phulkas for serving Method 1. Heat a nonstick pan. Add coriander seeds and cumin seeds and dry roast for 30 seconds. 2. Add black peppercorns, cloves, green cardamoms, black cardamoms, fennel seeds, mace, stone flower, lotus seeds, dried red chillies and star anise and sauté for 3-4 minutes or till fragrant. 3. Add poppy seeds, nutmeg and sauté for 30 seconds. 4. Transfer on a plate and allow to cool. 5. Heat a small pan. Add mustard oil and allow to smoke. Set aside to cool down. 6. Transfer the roasted mixture in a grinder jar, add salt and grind to fine powder. 7. Take mutton in a bowl. Add onions, green cardamoms, cloves, bay leaves, cinnamon, black cardamoms, star anise, Kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, turmeric powder, 2 tbsps prepared spice mix, ginger-garlic paste and mix well. 8. Add salt and ½ cup mustard oil and mix well. Set aside to marinate for 6-8 hours. 9. Preheat the oven at 160⁰C. 10. Add green chillies, dried red chillies and garlic pods and mix well. 11. Add mustard oil in earthenware pot and add marinated mutton on top. 12. Drizzle remaining mustard oil on top. 13. Seal the pot with refined flour dough, make a hole on one side for the steam to escape and place in the preheated oven. 14. Cook for 1 hour 15 minutes. Hold the pot with a cloth duster from both the sides and shake after every 15 minutes. 15. Seal the hole and set aside for 10-15 minutes. 16. Garnish with coriander sprig and serve hot with phulkas. ingredients: - Mutton- 500 gms (with fat and bones) | - Turmeric powder- 3/4 tsp | - Onions, sliced- 2 medium | - Tomatoes, sliced- 2 medium | - Ginger Garlic Paste - 2 tsp | - Salt- 1 tsp | - Whisked curd/yogurt- 1/2 cup | - Green chillies, chopped- 3 | - Ginger julienned- 1 small piece | - Refined oil- 3 tbsp | - Kasuri Methi (roasted and powdered)- 1.5 tsp | - Coriander seeds- 3 tsp | - Cumin seeds- 1 tsp | - Fennel seeds- 1 tsp | - Peppercorns- 1 tsp | - Whole red chillies- 4 | - Dry roast the items specified for Karahi Masala in a pan on low heat for 3 mins till the items are roasted and release flavour. Take it out on a plate and allow it to cool. | - Transfer to a grinder/ blender and grind it into a powder. This should give around 6 tsp of Karahi Masala which is sufficient for 1 kg mutton. We’ll use 3 tsp of this powder for 500 gms mutton.
instructions: - Heat oil in a kadai or pan. | Add the sliced onions, mix well and fry for a minute on medium heat. | - Add the mutton pieces, mix well and stir for a minute on medium heat. | - Add the ginger garlic paste, mix well and fry for a minute. | - Add the sliced tomatoes, salt and turmeric powder. | Mix well and fry on medium for a minute. | - Now reduce heat to low and cover & cook on low heat for around 15 mins till the onions & tomatoes have softened. | Open the lid and give a stir in between. | - Now add 250 ml water, give a mix and cover & cook on low heat for around 40 mins till the mutton pieces are tender. (Alternatively you can also transfer it to a pressure cooker and pressure cook for 4 whistles till the mutton is tender.) | - Once the mutton pieces are tender, lower heat and add the whisked curd/yogurt. | - Mix it well and cook on low heat for around 4-5 mins till it is cooked and oil separates. | - Add 3 tsp of the karahi masala that was made earlier and mix it well. | - Now add the chopped green chillies and ginger juliennes, mix and cook for 5 mins on low heat. | You can add little water if required. | - Add the roasted, powdered Kasuri Methi, mix it well and simmer for 2-3 mins. | Garnish with chopped coriander leaves.
raw_text: Karahi Mutton | Karahi Gosht | Mutton Karahi Restaurant Style | Kadai Mutton | Kadai Gosht | Kadhai Gosht | Karahi Gosht Recipe | Mutton Recipe Ingredients for Karahi Mutton: - Mutton- 500 gms (with fat and bones) - Turmeric powder- 3/4 tsp - Onions, sliced- 2 medium - Tomatoes, sliced- 2 medium - Ginger Garlic Paste - 2 tsp - Salt- 1 tsp - Whisked curd/yogurt- 1/2 cup - Green chillies, chopped- 3 - Ginger julienned- 1 small piece - Refined oil- 3 tbsp - Kasuri Methi (roasted and powdered)- 1.5 tsp To make Karahi Masala: - Coriander seeds- 3 tsp - Cumin seeds- 1 tsp - Fennel seeds- 1 tsp - Peppercorns- 1 tsp - Whole red chillies- 4 Preparation: - Dry roast the items specified for Karahi Masala in a pan on low heat for 3 mins till the items are roasted and release flavour. Take it out on a plate and allow it to cool. - Transfer to a grinder/ blender and grind it into a powder. This should give around 6 tsp of Karahi Masala which is sufficient for 1 kg mutton. We’ll use 3 tsp of this powder for 500 gms mutton. - Clean & wash the mutton pieces. - Slice the onions & tomatoes, chop the green chillies, cut an inch of ginger into juliennes and whisk the curd/yogurt. Keep little coriander to be chopped later for use as garnish. Process: - Heat oil in a kadai or pan. Add the sliced onions, mix well and fry for a minute on medium heat. - Add the mutton pieces, mix well and stir for a minute on medium heat. - Add the ginger garlic paste, mix well and fry for a minute. - Add the sliced tomatoes, salt and turmeric powder. Mix well and fry on medium for a minute. - Now reduce heat to low and cover & cook on low heat for around 15 mins till the onions & tomatoes have softened. Open the lid and give a stir in between. - Now add 250 ml water, give a mix and cover & cook on low heat for around 40 mins till the mutton pieces are tender. (Alternatively you can also transfer it to a pressure cooker and pressure cook for 4 whistles till the mutton is tender.) - Once the mutton pieces are tender, lower heat and add the whisked curd/yogurt. - Mix it well and cook on low heat for around 4-5 mins till it is cooked and oil separates. - Add 3 tsp of the karahi masala that was made earlier and mix it well. - Now add the chopped green chillies and ginger juliennes, mix and cook for 5 mins on low heat. You can add little water if required. - Add the roasted, powdered Kasuri Methi, mix it well and simmer for 2-3 mins. Garnish with chopped coriander leaves. #karahimutton #karahigosht #muttonkarahi #kadaimutton #muttonkarahirestaurantstyle #muttonsurry #spiceeats #spiceeatsrecipes #spiceeatsmutton

raw_text: Please take a moment to like and subscribe http://www.youtube.com/c/MadhurasRecipeMarathi?sub_confirmation=1 ✿◕ ‿ ◕✿ Mutton Curry is a non vegetarian delicacy. Soft chunks of meat in coconut base gravy. This is a perfect side dish. Let’s learn to make Mutton curry. Ingredients: For Marination: • 375 gm Mutton • 1/4 tsp Turmeric powder • 1 tsp Ginger-Garlic paste • 1/2 tsp Salt • Finely chopped Coriander leaves • 1 tsp Lemon juice • 1 glass Water For Curry Masala: • 1/4 cup Grated dry Coconut • 1 tsp Oil • Cumin seeds • Few Cloves • Few Black pepper • Cinnamon stick • 2 tsp white Sesame seeds • 1 tsp Poppy seeds • 2 medium sliced Onion For Curry: • 2 tsp Oil • 1 tsp Ginger-Garlic paste • 1 tsp Red chilli powder • Coriander powder • Salt to taste Method: For Marination: • Take meat and wash it really good under running water for 4-5 times. • Transfer it into pressure cooker to marinate. You can marinate it in separate bowl if you want. • Add turmeric powder, ginger-garlic paste, salt, coriander and lemon juice. Mix well. • Cover and marinate for at least an hour. • After an hour add water and mix well. • Close the lid and cook on medium heat until 7-8 whistles. • Try to cook on medium heat only as it takes longer time for mutton to cook. For Curry Masala: • Heat up a pan on medium heat and add coconut to it. Roast until coconut turns golden. Transfer the coconut into blender jar. • In same pan heat up oil. Add cumin seeds and let them sizzle up. • Add cloves, black pepper and cinnamon stick. Mix well and add white sesame seeds, poppy seeds. • Mix well and add sliced onion. Fry the onion on medium heat until it gets golden or brownish colour for about 7-8 minutes. • You also can add red chillies or coriander seeds in this masala if you want. • Turn off the gas and transfer this masala into blender jar having coconut. • Blend masala into fine paste by adding minimum water. • If you can make fine paste without adding any water that's best. For Curry: • Heat up oil in a pan. Add the masala in hot oil and mix well. • Add ginger-garlic paste. You also can add finely chopped ginger and garlic while frying the onion. • Add red chili powder and coriander powder. • Fry the masala on medium heat until it releases oil for about 5-6 minutes. • Add cooked mutton in this masala. Add a little more water. • Add salt. Keep the salt we added while cooking mutton and add accordingly. Mix well. • Cover and cook the mutton on medium heat for about 7-8 minutes. • Turn off the gas and mutton curry is already. • Mutton curry goes well with jawar or bajari or tandalachi bhakari. • You can also have this with rice. title: Basic Indian Lamb Curry | How To Make A Lamb Curry | Easy Lamb Curry
description: Basic Indian Lamb Curry | How To Make A Lamb Curry | Easy Lamb Curry | Lamb Curry Recipe | Mutton Curry | Indian Lamb Curry | Lamb Curry Indian Style | Lamb Curry Recipe Indian | Best Lamb Curry | Mutton Curry Indian Style | Easy Lamb Curry | Lamb Recipe Ingredients for Indian Lamb Curry: (Tsp-Teaspoon; Tbsp-Tablespoon) - Lamb - 2 lb (900 gms), curry cut with bones
ingredients: (Tsp-Teaspoon; Tbsp-Tablespoon) | - Lamb - 2 lb (900 gms), curry cut with bones | - Green Cardamom-6-8 | - Cloves-8-10 | - Cinnamon-2 | - Black Cardamom-3 | - Black peppercorns-3/4 tsp | - Cumin seeds- 1.5 tsp | - Dry Bay leaf-3 | - Turmeric powder-3/4 tsp | - Red Chilli Powder-3 tsp | - Coriander Powder- 3 tsp | - Garam Masala powder-3/4 -1 tsp | - Onion, sliced -4 medium (300 gms) | - Ginger garlic paste -4 tsp | - Tomatoes chopped- 4 medium (300 gms) | - Salt-2 tsp | - Oil -4 tbsp | - Ghee (clarified butter) - 2 tbsp
instructions: - Heat oil & ghee in a pan/kadhai and add all the whole spices. | - Give a stir to release the flavour and add the sliced onions. | - Fry on medium heat for around 10 mins till it’s light golden brown and then add the ginger garlic paste. | Mix and fry on low heat for around 2 mins and then add the lamb pieces. | - Mix and sear on high heat for around 5 mins till browned and then add all the spice powders and salt. | - Mix and sear on medium heat for around 2-3 mins. | You can add a splash of water if required. | - Now add the chopped tomatoes, mix and cook on medium heat for a min and then cover & cook on low heat for around 7-10 mins till the tomatoes are soft and pulpy. | Add 1/2 tsp garam masala power, mix and cook for 2 mins. | - Now add 500 ml water and cover & cook on low heat for around 30 mins | - Check the meat and add more water (around 200 ml) till the meat is tender. | Continue cooking on low heat for another 30 mins till meat is tender and oil separates. | - Add the sliced green chillies and chopped coriander leaves. | Cook on low heat for 2 mins | - Serve with rice or naan.
raw_text: Basic Indian Lamb Curry | How To Make A Lamb Curry | Easy Lamb Curry | Lamb Curry Recipe | Mutton Curry | Indian Lamb Curry | Lamb Curry Indian Style | Lamb Curry Recipe Indian | Best Lamb Curry | Mutton Curry Indian Style | Easy Lamb Curry | Lamb Recipe Ingredients for Indian Lamb Curry: (Tsp-Teaspoon; Tbsp-Tablespoon) - Lamb - 2 lb (900 gms), curry cut with bones Tempering: - Green Cardamom-6-8 - Cloves-8-10 - Cinnamon-2 - Black Cardamom-3 - Black peppercorns-3/4 tsp - Cumin seeds- 1.5 tsp - Dry Bay leaf-3 Spice Powders: - Turmeric powder-3/4 tsp - Red Chilli Powder-3 tsp - Coriander Powder- 3 tsp - Garam Masala powder-3/4 -1 tsp Other Ingredients: - Onion, sliced -4 medium (300 gms) - Ginger garlic paste -4 tsp - Tomatoes chopped- 4 medium (300 gms) - Salt-2 tsp - Oil -4 tbsp - Ghee (clarified butter) - 2 tbsp Process: - Heat oil & ghee in a pan/kadhai and add all the whole spices. - Give a stir to release the flavour and add the sliced onions. - Fry on medium heat for around 10 mins till it’s light golden brown and then add the ginger garlic paste. Mix and fry on low heat for around 2 mins and then add the lamb pieces. - Mix and sear on high heat for around 5 mins till browned and then add all the spice powders and salt. - Mix and sear on medium heat for around 2-3 mins. You can add a splash of water if required. - Now add the chopped tomatoes, mix and cook on medium heat for a min and then cover & cook on low heat for around 7-10 mins till the tomatoes are soft and pulpy. Add 1/2 tsp garam masala power, mix and cook for 2 mins. - Now add 500 ml water and cover & cook on low heat for around 30 mins - Check the meat and add more water (around 200 ml) till the meat is tender. Continue cooking on low heat for another 30 mins till meat is tender and oil separates. - Add the sliced green chillies and chopped coriander leaves. Cook on low heat for 2 mins - Serve with rice or naan. #indianlambcurry #lambcurry #muttoncurry #muttoncurryrecipe #lambcurryrecipe #lambrecipe #spiceeats #spiceeatsrecipes

title: Mughlai Mutton Curry | Slow Cooked Authentic Indian Mutton Recipe Step by Step | Smita Deo
description: Mughlai Mutton Curry | Rich, Aromatic and Full of Flavour | Smita Deo Learn how to make an authentic, deeply aromatic Mughlai Mutton Curry, slow-cooked mutton in a bold Kashmiri red chilli, fresh mint and onion masala, finished with ghee and whisked yogurt for a rich, restaurant-style curry that is impossible to forget. This is not an everyday curry. This is the kind of dish that fills the entire house with aroma, stops every conversation at the dinner table and has everyone reaching for more! ------------------------------------------
ingredients: 15 dry Kashmiri red chillies | 15-20 fresh mint leaves | 6-7 large garlic cloves | 1 inch cinnamon | 1 tsp cumin seeds | 1 cup water | 1 inch ginger | 5 green cardamoms | 6-7 cloves | 10-15 peppercorns | 3 large onions, roughly chopped | 1/2 cup ghee | 1/2 tsp turmeric powder | 1 cup yogurt, whisked | 1 kg mutton | 3 cups water | Salt to taste
instructions: Step 1 – Prepare the Red Chilli Mixture: | In a small kadhai, combine dry red chillies, fresh mint leaves, garlic cloves, cinnamon, cumin seeds and 1 cup water. | Bring to a boil and cook until the chillies soften completely. | Remove from heat and allow to cool fully before grinding. | Step 2 – Grind the Masala: | Add ginger, green cardamoms, cloves, peppercorns and roughly chopped onions to a grinder. | Add the cooled chilli and mint mixture. | Grind everything together into a fine, smooth paste. | Do not add extra water, the moisture from the mixture is sufficient. | Step 3 – Fry the Masala in Ghee: | Heat 1/2 cup ghee in a heavy-bottom pan. | Add the ground masala and fry patiently on medium flame, stirring regularly, until the masala darkens slightly in colour and the raw smell disappears completely. | This step is the heart of the recipe, do not rush it. | Step 4 – Add the Yogurt: | Add turmeric powder and mix well. | Reduce the flame to the lowest setting before adding the yogurt. | Add the whisked yogurt gradually and cook slowly, stirring continuously, until the oil starts separating from the masala. | This low and slow technique is what gives the gravy its silky, rich texture. | Step 5 – Cook the Mutton: | Add the mutton pieces and cook on high flame for 4-5 minutes, stirring to coat every piece with the masala. | Add 3 cups water and salt to taste. | Cover and cook on medium-low flame until the mutton is completely tender and the oil floats on top, the clearest sign that the curry is ready. | Serving Suggestion: Serve piping hot with naan, roomali roti, paratha, kulcha or steamed basmati rice. | Garnish with fresh mint leaves for an extra burst of freshness. | ------------------------------------------ | * Use Kashmiri red chillies specifically - they give a deep red colour without making the curry excessively spicy | * Boil the chillies with mint till completely soft before grinding - this is what gives the masala its depth and vibrancy | * Never add extra water while grinding - a thick, concentrated paste is the key to a rich curry | * Fry the masala patiently in ghee till it darkens - this step cannot be rushed | * Always add yogurt on the lowest flame - high heat will cause it to split and curdle instantly | * Bhunoo the mutton on high flame before adding water - this seals the juices and prevents the meat from becoming dry | * The ghee floating on top is the clearest sign that the curry is perfectly cooked, do not drain it | ------------------------------------------ | Tried this Mughlai Mutton Curry recipe? | Drop a comment below, I would love to hear how it turned out! | Don't forget to Like, Share, and Subscribe for more authentic Indian home cooking recipes every week with Smita Deo! | ------------------------------------------ | (mughlai mutton curry | mutton curry recipe | indian mutton gravy | rich mutton curry | homestyle mughlai recipes | Smita Deo recipes)
raw_text: Mughlai Mutton Curry | Rich, Aromatic and Full of Flavour | Smita Deo Learn how to make an authentic, deeply aromatic Mughlai Mutton Curry, slow-cooked mutton in a bold Kashmiri red chilli, fresh mint and onion masala, finished with ghee and whisked yogurt for a rich, restaurant-style curry that is impossible to forget. This is not an everyday curry. This is the kind of dish that fills the entire house with aroma, stops every conversation at the dinner table and has everyone reaching for more! ------------------------------------------ INGREDIENTS: For the Chilli and Mint Base: 15 dry Kashmiri red chillies 15-20 fresh mint leaves 6-7 large garlic cloves 1 inch cinnamon 1 tsp cumin seeds 1 cup water For the Masala Paste: 1 inch ginger 5 green cardamoms 6-7 cloves 10-15 peppercorns 3 large onions, roughly chopped For the Curry: 1/2 cup ghee 1/2 tsp turmeric powder 1 cup yogurt, whisked 1 kg mutton 3 cups water Salt to taste ------------------------------------------ METHOD OF PREPARATION: Step 1 – Prepare the Red Chilli Mixture: In a small kadhai, combine dry red chillies, fresh mint leaves, garlic cloves, cinnamon, cumin seeds and 1 cup water. Bring to a boil and cook until the chillies soften completely. Remove from heat and allow to cool fully before grinding. Step 2 – Grind the Masala: Add ginger, green cardamoms, cloves, peppercorns and roughly chopped onions to a grinder. Add the cooled chilli and mint mixture. Grind everything together into a fine, smooth paste. Do not add extra water, the moisture from the mixture is sufficient. Step 3 – Fry the Masala in Ghee: Heat 1/2 cup ghee in a heavy-bottom pan. Add the ground masala and fry patiently on medium flame, stirring regularly, until the masala darkens slightly in colour and the raw smell disappears completely. This step is the heart of the recipe, do not rush it. Step 4 – Add the Yogurt: Add turmeric powder and mix well. Reduce the flame to the lowest setting before adding the yogurt. Add the whisked yogurt gradually and cook slowly, stirring continuously, until the oil starts separating from the masala. This low and slow technique is what gives the gravy its silky, rich texture. Step 5 – Cook the Mutton: Add the mutton pieces and cook on high flame for 4-5 minutes, stirring to coat every piece with the masala. Add 3 cups water and salt to taste. Mix well. Cover and cook on medium-low flame until the mutton is completely tender and the oil floats on top, the clearest sign that the curry is ready. Serving Suggestion: Serve piping hot with naan, roomali roti, paratha, kulcha or steamed basmati rice. Garnish with fresh mint leaves for an extra burst of freshness. ------------------------------------------ PRO TIPS: * Use Kashmiri red chillies specifically - they give a deep red colour without making the curry excessively spicy * Boil the chillies with mint till completely soft before grinding - this is what gives the masala its depth and vibrancy * Never add extra water while grinding - a thick, concentrated paste is the key to a rich curry * Fry the masala patiently in ghee till it darkens - this step cannot be rushed * Always add yogurt on the lowest flame - high heat will cause it to split and curdle instantly * Bhunoo the mutton on high flame before adding water - this seals the juices and prevents the meat from becoming dry * The ghee floating on top is the clearest sign that the curry is perfectly cooked, do not drain it ------------------------------------------ Tried this Mughlai Mutton Curry recipe? Drop a comment below, I would love to hear how it turned out! Don't forget to Like, Share, and Subscribe for more authentic Indian home cooking recipes every week with Smita Deo! ------------------------------------------ (mughlai mutton curry mutton curry recipe indian mutton gravy rich mutton curry homestyle mughlai recipes Smita Deo recipes) #MughlaiMuttonCurry #MuttonCurryRecipe #SmitaDeo #SmitaDeoRecipes #KashmiriMuttonCurry #GheeRoastMutton #IndianMuttonGravy #RichMuttonCurry #AuthenticMughlaiFood #MuttonLovers #IndianMuttonRecipe #MintMuttonCurry #SlowCookedMutton #MuttonGravy #HomestyleMutton #WeekendCooking #IndianCooking #HomeCooking #MuttonWithParatha #TraditionalIndianCurry

title: SIMPLE MUTTON BIRYANI FOR BEGINNERS | MUTTON BIRYANI WITH BIRYANI MASALA | MUTTON BIRYANI RECIPE
description: Simple Mutton Biryani for Beginners | Mutton Biryani with Biryani Masala | Mutton Biryani Recipe | How To Make Mutton Biryani | Easy Mutton Biryani Recipe | Mutton Biryani Recipe for Bachelors | Simple Mutton Biryani for Bachelors | | Mutton Biryani Ingredients for Mutton Biryani: - Mutton (or Goat Meat), large pieces with bones- 1 kg - Basmati Rice- 700 gms (4 US cup measure)
ingredients: - Mutton (or Goat Meat), large pieces with bones- 1 kg | - Basmati Rice- 700 gms (4 US cup measure) | - Ginger Garlic paste- 2 tbsp | - Turmeric Powder- 1/2 tsp | - Red Chilli Powder- 1 tsp | - Ready made Biryani Masala- 5 tbsp | - Whisked curd/ plain yogurt- 1 US cup measure | - Green Chillies, slit- 5 numbers | - Coriander leaves, chopped- 1 cup | - Mint leaves, chopped- 1 cup | - Refined Oil- 5-6 tbsp | - Green Cardamom- 5 | - Cloves- 5 | - Cinnamon- 3 | - Onions sliced- 5 medium (400 gms) + 1 medium to be made into a birista | ( since Biryani Masala has salt added to it, no salt has been added while cooking the mutton. Do taste the gravy for salt while cooking the mutton and add salt if and as required) | - Shahjeera (caraway seeds) - 3/4 tsp | - Green cardamom - 4 | - Cloves - 4 | - Cinnamon- 2 pieces | - Salt- 2 1/2 tablespoon | - Water to boil the rice- around 2.5 litres | - Mint leaves - around 10- 15 leaves | - Marinate the mutton with the items indicated and set aside for 1 hour. | - In case not using pressure cooker, marinate for 4 hours or overnight in a refrigerator. | - Slice the onions ( all the 6 onions) and slit the green chillies | - Chop 1 cup coriander leaves and 1 cup mint | - Wash & soak the Basmati Rice 30 mins before you cook it.
instructions: To cook the Mutton : | - Heat 5-6 tbsp oil in a pressure cooker or heavy bottom pan and add the whole spices. | - Give a stir and when it splutters, add the 5 medium sized sliced onions. | - Fry on high heat for around 12 mins till the onions are light brown. | - Now add the marinated mutton, mix and fry on high heat for 3 mins. | - Now continue to fry the mutton on medium heat for another 5 mins till oil separates. (Readymade Biryani Masala usually contains salt, hence salt is not added while cooking the mutton in this recipe. | Do check if the biryani masala you are using has salt in it or else add as per your taste.) | - Add 150 ml water, give a mix and close the pressure cooker. | In case the pressure cooker has a whistle cook for 3 whistles ( on high heat till 1st whistle and on low for next 2) or for 15 mins when using a different kind of pressure cooker. | - In case you’re not using a pressure cooker, continue to cook covered on low heat for 45-60 mins till meat is tender and add water as & when required. | Give a stir few times so that it doesn’t get stuck at the bottom. | - Once the pressure has released, open the lid & dry up any excess water to get a thick gravy. | To cook the Rice: | - Take one more heavy bottom pan & add around 2.5 litres of water. | Place it on high heat. | - You may include the water in which rice was soaked in this 2.5 litres for enhanced flavour & taste. | - Once the water starts to boil or bubble, add Shah Jeera - 3/4 tsp, Green cardamom -4, Cloves - 4, Cinnamon- 2 pieces and Salt- 2 1/2 tablespoon (two and half tablespoon) | - Stir it well to dissolve the salt completely . | The water must be very salty. | - Once the water starts boiling, add the strained basmati rice & stir it well. | - Continue to boil it on high heat, stirring few times very gently to not break the rice. | - After around 8-9 minutes when the basmati rice is almost done (90%) remove it from heat and strain the water. | Set it aside. | Layering the rice and cooked chicken: | - Take a heavy bottom pan and spread half the cooked rice as the 1st layer. | - Now spread the cooked mutton with gravy on top of the rice uniformly as shown in the video. | - Next, spread the balance rice as the top layer. | - Sprinkle the birista & mint leaves on top of the rice. | Close the lid and cook on low heat for 7-8 mins. | - Allow it to rest for around 15 mins. | - Once you open the lid, mix the meat and rice evenly to serve.
raw_text: Simple Mutton Biryani for Beginners | Mutton Biryani with Biryani Masala | Mutton Biryani Recipe | How To Make Mutton Biryani | Easy Mutton Biryani Recipe | Mutton Biryani Recipe for Bachelors | Simple Mutton Biryani for Bachelors | | Mutton Biryani Ingredients for Mutton Biryani: - Mutton (or Goat Meat), large pieces with bones- 1 kg - Basmati Rice- 700 gms (4 US cup measure) For Marinating the Mutton: - Ginger Garlic paste- 2 tbsp - Turmeric Powder- 1/2 tsp - Red Chilli Powder- 1 tsp - Ready made Biryani Masala- 5 tbsp - Whisked curd/ plain yogurt- 1 US cup measure - Green Chillies, slit- 5 numbers - Coriander leaves, chopped- 1 cup - Mint leaves, chopped- 1 cup For Cooking the Mutton: - Refined Oil- 5-6 tbsp - Green Cardamom- 5 - Cloves- 5 - Cinnamon- 3 - Onions sliced- 5 medium (400 gms) + 1 medium to be made into a birista ( since Biryani Masala has salt added to it, no salt has been added while cooking the mutton. Do taste the gravy for salt while cooking the mutton and add salt if and as required) For Cooking Rice: - Shahjeera (caraway seeds) - 3/4 tsp - Green cardamom - 4 - Cloves - 4 - Cinnamon- 2 pieces - Salt- 2 1/2 tablespoon - Water to boil the rice- around 2.5 litres - Mint leaves - around 10- 15 leaves Preparation : - Marinate the mutton with the items indicated and set aside for 1 hour. - In case not using pressure cooker, marinate for 4 hours or overnight in a refrigerator. - Slice the onions ( all the 6 onions) and slit the green chillies - Fry one sliced onion into a birista till golden, set aside for use as a garnish. - Chop 1 cup coriander leaves and 1 cup mint - Whisk the yogurt and set aside - Wash & soak the Basmati Rice 30 mins before you cook it. Process : To cook the Mutton : - Heat 5-6 tbsp oil in a pressure cooker or heavy bottom pan and add the whole spices. - Give a stir and when it splutters, add the 5 medium sized sliced onions. - Fry on high heat for around 12 mins till the onions are light brown. - Now add the marinated mutton, mix and fry on high heat for 3 mins. - Now continue to fry the mutton on medium heat for another 5 mins till oil separates. (Readymade Biryani Masala usually contains salt, hence salt is not added while cooking the mutton in this recipe. Do check if the biryani masala you are using has salt in it or else add as per your taste.) - Add 150 ml water, give a mix and close the pressure cooker. In case the pressure cooker has a whistle cook for 3 whistles ( on high heat till 1st whistle and on low for next 2) or for 15 mins when using a different kind of pressure cooker. - In case you’re not using a pressure cooker, continue to cook covered on low heat for 45-60 mins till meat is tender and add water as & when required. Give a stir few times so that it doesn’t get stuck at the bottom. - Once the pressure has released, open the lid & dry up any excess water to get a thick gravy. To cook the Rice: - Take one more heavy bottom pan & add around 2.5 litres of water. Place it on high heat. - You may include the water in which rice was soaked in this 2.5 litres for enhanced flavour & taste. - Once the water starts to boil or bubble, add Shah Jeera - 3/4 tsp, Green cardamom -4, Cloves - 4, Cinnamon- 2 pieces and Salt- 2 1/2 tablespoon (two and half tablespoon) - Stir it well to dissolve the salt completely . The water must be very salty. - Once the water starts boiling, add the strained basmati rice & stir it well. - Continue to boil it on high heat, stirring few times very gently to not break the rice. - After around 8-9 minutes when the basmati rice is almost done (90%) remove it from heat and strain the water. Set it aside. Layering the rice and cooked chicken: - Take a heavy bottom pan and spread half the cooked rice as the 1st layer. - Now spread the cooked mutton with gravy on top of the rice uniformly as shown in the video. - Next, spread the balance rice as the top layer. - Sprinkle the birista & mint leaves on top of the rice. Close the lid and cook on low heat for 7-8 mins. - Allow it to rest for around 15 mins. - Once you open the lid, mix the meat and rice evenly to serve. #muttonbiryani #simplemuttonbiryani #easymuttonbiryani #muttonbiryanirecipe #bachelormuttonbiryani #spiceeats #spiceeatsrecipes #spiceeatsmutton

title: Railway Mutton Curry | 100 Year Old Anglo-Indian Mutton Recipe | Historical Mutton Curry
description: Railway Mutton Curry is an authentic famous British Colonial Era Dish, which was served to First Class compartment passengers in long distance trains in India. This 100 year old Anglo-Indian Mutton Recipe, has it's Legacy of the British Raj and it was very popular among the Britishers and the Indians. This recipe is a milder version of the famous Bengali Mutton recipe " Mangshor Jhol " or " Kosha Mangsho" . The Masalas and the Coconut milk used in this famous recipe is the game changer.This extremely popular British Bengali mutton / lamb dish, is now a lost recipe. Preparation Time : 10 Min Cooking Time : 30 min ( with Pressure cooker ) Total Time : 40 min
ingredients: Mutton or Lamb - 600 Grams | Curd - 2 Tblspn | Ginger Garlic paste : 2 Tspn | Turmeric Powder - 2 Tspn | Salt and Sugar - according to Taste | Ghee -1 Tspn | Garam Masala - 1 Tspn | Coconut Milk - 100 ml | Chopped Onion - 3 medium Size | Mustard Oil - 6 Tspn | Potato - 3 medium size, cut into halves | Water - 1 glass
instructions: Marinate the Mutton or Lamb with Curd, Salt, Turmeric powder and red Chilli powder. | Marinate it for an hour. | Heat the mustard oil. | Fry the potatoes with little salt & turmeric powder. | Take them out and keep aside when they turn golden brown in color. | Now add the whole spices and saute. | Add the chopped onion and when it turns translucent add the Ginger Garlic paste. | Add the chopped Tomato and stir well. | When the tomato turns soft add salt, Turmeric Powder, Red Chilli Powder and Sugar. | Now add the marinated mutton. | Fry it for 2 minutes and add the salt and green chillies. | Cook it for another 15 minutes . | Add the dry roasted special railway mutton masala. | Add the fried potatoes and cook for a minute mixing all the ingredients together. | Add a glass of water. | Now cook the mutton in a Pressure Cooker. | Allow 3-4 whistles until the Mutton / Lamb turns tender. | Open the Lid of the pressure cooker and add coconut milk, Garam Masala powder and Ghee. | Serve it hot with Rice or any Indian Bread like Naan or Chapati | Please visit our website : | http://www.sanchitaskitchen.com/ | Please Like Our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/SanchitaKitc... | Instagram : https://www.instagram.com/sanchitaski... | music used:https://www.bensound.com/Music used: | https://www.bensound.com/
raw_text: Railway Mutton Curry is an authentic famous British Colonial Era Dish, which was served to First Class compartment passengers in long distance trains in India. This 100 year old Anglo-Indian Mutton Recipe, has it's Legacy of the British Raj and it was very popular among the Britishers and the Indians. This recipe is a milder version of the famous Bengali Mutton recipe " Mangshor Jhol " or " Kosha Mangsho" . The Masalas and the Coconut milk used in this famous recipe is the game changer.This extremely popular British Bengali mutton / lamb dish, is now a lost recipe. Preparation Time : 10 Min Cooking Time : 30 min ( with Pressure cooker ) Total Time : 40 min Serves - 4 Ingredients : Mutton or Lamb - 600 Grams Curd - 2 Tblspn Ginger Garlic paste : 2 Tspn Turmeric Powder - 2 Tspn Red Chilli Powder Salt and Sugar - according to Taste Ghee -1 Tspn Garam Masala - 1 Tspn Coconut Milk - 100 ml Chopped Onion - 3 medium Size Mustard Oil - 6 Tspn Potato - 3 medium size, cut into halves Whole Spices - Bay leaves, green and black cardamon, Cinnamon Sticks, Cumin Seeds . Special Railway Mutton Masala : Cumin Seed, Coriander Seed, Fennel Seed, and Black Pepper corns roasted and grounded together. Water - 1 glass Method Of Cooking : Step -1 Marinate the Mutton or Lamb with Curd, Salt, Turmeric powder and red Chilli powder. Marinate it for an hour. Step - 2 Heat the mustard oil. Fry the potatoes with little salt & turmeric powder. Take them out and keep aside when they turn golden brown in color. Now add the whole spices and saute. Add the chopped onion and when it turns translucent add the Ginger Garlic paste. Add the chopped Tomato and stir well. Step -3 When the tomato turns soft add salt, Turmeric Powder, Red Chilli Powder and Sugar. Now add the marinated mutton. Fry it for 2 minutes and add the salt and green chillies. Cook it for another 15 minutes . Add the dry roasted special railway mutton masala. Add the fried potatoes and cook for a minute mixing all the ingredients together. Add a glass of water. Step - 4 Now cook the mutton in a Pressure Cooker. Allow 3-4 whistles until the Mutton / Lamb turns tender. Open the Lid of the pressure cooker and add coconut milk, Garam Masala powder and Ghee. instructions: To the mutton, add turmeric powder, chilli powder, pepper, salt and mix well. | Let this sit for 1 hours. | To the pressure cooker, add the mutton pieces, ginger garlic paste and water. | Pressure cook on low flame for 3 whistle. | To a pan, add oil, whole spices, onion and saute until they are brown in color. | Add green chillies and continue to saute. | Now add chopped tomatoes and salt, and cook. | Once the tomatoes are soft, add the cooked mutton with the water. | Cook until moisture evaporates. | Add coriander powder, and cook until the masala give thick. | Finally add ghee, coriander leaves. | Mutton masala is ready. | You can buy our book and classes on https://www.21frames.in/shop | HAPPY COOKING WITH HOMECOOKING | ENJOY OUR RECIPES
raw_text: 📌 Authentic & flavorful mutton dishes 🍖 👉 https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLFhw-Rie62rQmRX_I-JMqlzlGzRX11uKI Mutton Masala | Mutton Recipe | Boneless Mutton Masala Gravy | Mutton Curry | Spicy #MuttonMasala #Goshtkasalan #spicymuttonmasala #GoshtKaSalan #muttongravy #MuttonRecipe #muttonkarikolambu #BonelessMuttonMasala #Homecooking #Mutton #muttonlajawab #spicytadkamuttonrecipe #muttoncurry #howtomake #MuttonKorma #recipevideo #muttonkulambu #tasty #delicious #yummy #howtocook #spicy Ingredients Boneless Mutton - 1 Kg Turmeric Powder - 1 Tsp (Buy: https://amzn.to/2RC4fm4) Chilli Powder - 3 Tsp (Buy: https://amzn.to/3b4yHyg) Pepper - 1/2 Tsp (Buy: https://amzn.to/2RElrrg) Salt - 1 Tsp (Buy: https://amzn.to/2vg124l) Ginger Garlic Paste - 2 Tsp (Buy: https://amzn.to/314FymX) Water - 1/2 Cup Oil - 2 Tbsp (Buy: https://amzn.to/2RGYvrw) Whole Spices (Bay Leaf, Cinnamon, Clove & Cardamom) Onion - 4 Nos Chopped Green Chilli - 3 Nos Slit Tomato - 4 Nos Chopped Salt - 1/2 Tsp (Buy: https://amzn.to/2vg124l) Coriander Powder - 2 Tsp (Buy: https://amzn.to/36nEgEq) Garam Masala - 1/2 Tsp (Buy: https://amzn.to/2TPe8jd) Ghee - 2 Tsp (Optional) (Buy: https://amzn.to/2RBvKxw) Chopped Coriander Leaves Method: 1. To the mutton, add turmeric powder, chilli powder, pepper, salt and mix well. 2. Let this sit for 1 hours. 3. To the pressure cooker, add the mutton pieces, ginger garlic paste and water. 4. Pressure cook on low flame for 3 whistle. 5. To a pan, add oil, whole spices, onion and saute until they are brown in color. 6. Add green chillies and continue to saute. 7. Now add chopped tomatoes and salt, and cook. 8. Once the tomatoes are soft, add the cooked mutton with the water. 9. Cook until moisture evaporates. 10. Add coriander powder, and cook until the masala give thick. 11. Finally add ghee, coriander leaves. 12. Mutton masala is ready. raw_text: Its hard to resist finishing these Tandoori mutton Chops. #HappyCookingToyou #ShanFoods Written Recipe: http://bit.ly/2NfgvIN Visit Our Website: https://www.foodfusion.com Download iOS & Android app: https://www.foodfusion.com/app Facebook: https://facebook.com/foodfusionpk Instagram: https://instagram.com/foodfusionpk Twitter: https://twitter.com/foodfusionpk Also Follow @HealthyFoodFusion & @FoodFusionKids for healthy food & kids special recipes. Tandoori Mutton Chops Serves 4-5 Recipe in English: Ingredients: -Mutton chops ½ kg -Pyaz (Onion) chopped 1 small -Adrak lehsan paste (Ginger garlic paste) 1 & ½ tbs -Namak (Salt) ½ tsp or to taste -Haldee powder (Turmeric powder) ½ tsp -Pani (Water) 1 & ½ Cups or as required -Dhania powder (Coriander powder) ½ tbs -Lal mirch powder (Red chilli powder) 1 tsp or to taste -Garam masala powder ½ tsp -Javatri powder (Mace powder) 1/4 tsp -Jaifil powder (Nutmeg powder) 1/4 tsp -Zeera (Cumin seeds) roasted & crushed 1 tsp -Hari mirch (Green chilli) paste 1 tbs -Lemon juice 1 tbs -Dahi (Yogurt) ½ Cup -Hara dhania (Fresh coriander) chopped 2 tbs -Tandoori masala 1 tbs -Cooking oil 3 tbs -Koyla (Charcoal) for smoke Directions: -In pot,add mutton chops,onion,ginger garlic paste,salt,turmeric powder and water,mix well and bring it to boil,cover and cook on low flame for 25-30 minutes or until tender then cook on high flame until dries up. -Let it rest for 6-8 minutes. -In bowl,add coriander powder,red chilli powder,garam masala powder,mace powder,nutmeg powder, cumin seeds,green chilli paste,lemon juice,yogurt,fresh coriander,tandoori masala and mix well. -Add cooked mutton chops and coat well,cover and marinate for 30 minutes. -In frying pan,add cooking oil,marinated mutton chops with remaining marinade,cover and cook on medium low flame for 5 minutes then cook uncovered from both sides until golden brown. -Give a charcoal for 2 minutes. Recipe in Urdu: Ajza: -Mutton chops ½ kg -Pyaz (Onion) chopped 1 small -Adrak lehsan paste (Ginger garlic paste) 1 & ½ tbs -Namak (Salt) ½ tsp or to taste -Haldee powder (Turmeric powder) ½ tsp -Pani (Water) 1 & ½ Cups or as required -Dhania powder (Coriander powder) ½ tbs -Lal mirch powder (Red chilli powder) 1 tsp or to taste -Garam masala powder ½ tsp -Javatri powder (Mace powder) 1/4 tsp -Jaifil powder (Nutmeg powder) 1/4 tsp -Zeera (Cumin seeds) roasted & crushed 1 tsp -Hari mirch (Green chilli) paste 1 tbs -Lemon juice 1 tbs -Dahi (Yogurt) ½ Cup -Hara dhania (Fresh coriander) chopped 2 tbs -Tandoori masala 1 tbs -Cooking oil 3 tbs -Koyla (Charcoal) for smoke Directions: -Pot mein mutton chops,pyaz,adrak lehsan paste,namak,haldee powder aur pani dal ker ache tarhan mix karein aur ubal anay ka bad dhak dein aur halki ancch per 25-30 minutes ya gul janay tak paka lein phir tez ancch per sukh janay tak paka lein. -6-8 minutes kliya rest dein. -Bowl mein dhania powder,lal mirch powder,garam masala powder,javatri powder,jaifil powder,zeera, hari mirch paste,lemin juice,dahi,hara dhania aur tandoori masala dal ker ache tarhan mix karein. -Cooked mutton chops dal ker ache tarhan coat karein aur dhak ker 30 minutes kliya marinate ker lein. -Frying pan mein cooking oil aur marinated mutton chops marinade ka saath dal dein aur dhak ker darmiyani halki ancch per 5 minutes kliya paka lein phir uncovered dono sides sa golden brown hunay tak paka lein. -2 minutes kliya koyla ka dhuwan dein.

title: SECRETS To Cooking A PERFECT Indian Style CHICKEN CURRY (STEP BY STEP INSTRUCTIONS)
description: How To Cook Indian Style Chicken Curry - Chicken Curry Recipe For Beginners If you follow this video step by step then I assure you that you will be making the best Indian style chicken curry. I tried my best to explain everything in detail and all the tips and tricks possible that go into making chicken curry. RECIPE HERE - https://bumbicurry.blogspot.com/2025/01/chicken-curry-indian-style.html Ingredients for making a chicken curry - serves 6 to 7
ingredients: * 2.2 lb/ 1 kg chicken on bones. Please note that I preferably use chicken on bones for making a chicken curry. It's not that you cannot use boneless chicken but the gravy won't be as tasty. | * 2 large potatoes quartered or medium potatoes halved. I did not boil the potatoes before adding them. | * 1 very large onion or 2 medium sized onions. I used quarter of the onion for making a paste. | * 2 inch length ginger | * 10 large garlic cloves | * 1 large tomato | * 2 green chillies (optional) | * 2 large dry bay leaf | * 1 inch length cinnamon stick | * 6 to 7 green cardamoms | * 4 to 5 cloves | * 1/4 th cup plain unflavored yogurt | * 1 teaspoon turmeric powder | * 2 heaped teaspoons Kashmiri red chilli powder. You can even use regular red chilli powder but in that case please use less. Adjust the proportion of any chilli powder according to your tolerance of heat level. Add only half a teaspoon to start with, do a taste test at the end and add more if you feel you want more chilli heat. | * 1 tablespoon ground coriander | * 1 teaspoon garam masala powder.If you want to make garam masala powder at home then here is the recipe - https://youtu.be/ciR43perZbU | * 1/4 th teaspoon ground mace (optional) | * 1/4 th teaspoon ground nutmeg (optional) | * 2 teaspoons salt (add less to start with, you can always add later) | * 1 teaspoon sugar(optional) | * 1 cup hot water. raw_text: How Make A Base Gravy Masala - Base Gravy Recipe 🔔Please click on the link below and subscribe to Curries With Bumbi so that I can bring more new recipes for you- https://www.youtube.com/@UCc5O8GaMvAGjmvENEvvqZrg Ingredients Required For Making Base Gravy Recipe - *570 gm/4 large onions * 675 gm/ 6 large tomatoes * 40 gm/ 3 inch length/ 1 tablespoon grated ginger * 40 gm/ 12 large garlic cloves or about 1 & 1/2 tablespoon finely grated * 1 green chilli * 30 gm/ 15 cashew nuts soaked in 1/3 rd cup hot water * 1 & 1/2 teaspoons cumin seeds * 2 -3 dry bay leaves * 2 inch length cinnamon stick * 5 to 6 green cardamoms * 1 & 1/2 teaspoons salt or to taste * 1/2 cup oil For spice paste * 1 tsp turmeric powder * 1 tsp cumin powder * 1 tablespoon Kashmiri Chili powder (please adjust proportion according to your preference) * 1 tablespoon coriander powder * 1 tablespoon of garam masala powder * 1/3 rd cup water METHOD - * In a bowl add turmeric powder, cumin powder, Kashmiri chili powder, coriander powder and garam masala powder. Add about a third of a cup of water, mix * Heat oil over medium heat * add dry bay leaves, cinnamon stick, green cardamoms and cumin seeds. Allow the seeds to splutter to release their flavor and then add chopped onions along with a fourth of a teaspoon of salt. Stir the onions on medium heat till they turn slightly brown in color. * Add the spice paste and fry on low heat for 2 to 3 mins * Add chopped green chilli, finely grated ginger and garlic. Fry on medium heat for a few minutes until you see the oil floating on top. * add crushed tomatoes along with half a teaspoon of salt. Stir on medium heat from time to time till the tomatoes get well fried and cooked and again the oil starts leaving the sides. * make a smooth cashew nut paste in your blender for which soak some cashew nut in hot water for about an hour *Add the cashew nut paste only after you see the tomatoes and onions have got well cooked and mixed together to become one single mass * Once again when you start seeing that oil oozing out along the sides or floating on top your base gravy is done. * You can store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for about a week or you can divide them into portions and it stores well in the freezer for 6 months. title: The Only Lamb Curry Recipe You’ll Ever Need
description: This lamb curry recipe is one of the tastiest you’ll ever make, yet it requires minimal effort—honestly, it’s almost too good to be true. Written Recipe: https://burmawalakitchen.com/recipe/effortless-lamb-curry/ My Equipment: Our Spice Blend: https://tinyurl.com/mww4573j
ingredients: o 1 kg bone-in lamb | o 1 tbsp salt | o 1 tbsp garam masala | o 1 tsp crushed black peppercorns | o 1 tsp turmeric | o 1 tbsp chili powder | o 2 tbsp Kashmiri red chili powder (for color and gentle heat) | o 2 tbsp each of ginger & garlic paste | o 1 cup desiccated coconut | o 1 cup crushed fried onions | o 3 tbsp yogurt (to tenderize the meat) | o ¼ cup oil | o ¼ cup oil | o 1 tbsp ghee | o 2 cups water | o 3 tbsp tomato purée | o 2 tbsp kasuri methi (crushed) | o 3-4 large green chilies (whole or slit) | o Generous handful of fresh coriander
instructions: Marinate the Lamb: | o In a large bowl, mix the lamb with salt, garam masala, black pepper, turmeric, chili powders, garlic-ginger paste, coconut, fried onions, yogurt, and oil. | o Massage everything in well, ensuring the meat is fully coated in this fragrant spice mix. | o If you can, let it rest in the fridge for at least an hour—it makes a world of difference! | Sear the Meat: | o Heat ¼ cup oil and 1 tbsp ghee in a pan over medium heat. | o Add the marinated lamb, making sure to scrape in every last bit of spice and yogurt. | o Let it sear for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking. | o The key here is patience—a beautiful crust means deep, rich flavours. | Build the Curry Base: | o Once the lamb is nicely seared, pour in 2 cups of water from the side of the pan—gently, so you don’t wash away the spices. | o Give it a stir, bring it to a gentle boil, then cover and simmer on low heat for 90 minutes. | o No extra frying, no unnecessary steps—just let time do the work. | Halfway Check-In: | o At the 45-minute mark, check on your curry. | o The oil will have started to rise, the aroma will be out of this world, but the meat will need more time—so we let it continue simmering. | Final Transformation: | o After 90 minutes, the lamb is melt-in-your-mouth tender, the gravy is thick and glossy, and the colour is deep and rich. | o Stir in 3 tbsp tomato purée, cooking for a couple of minutes to add a natural sweetness and deepen the colour even more. | Finishing Touches: | o Crush 2 tbsp kasuri methi into the curry for a beautiful earthy aroma. | o Add whole or slit green chilies for a fresh kick. | o Scatter over a generous handful of coriander—because no good curry is complete without it.
raw_text: This lamb curry recipe is one of the tastiest you’ll ever make, yet it requires minimal effort—honestly, it’s almost too good to be true. Written Recipe: https://burmawalakitchen.com/recipe/effortless-lamb-curry/ My Equipment: Our Spice Blend: https://tinyurl.com/mww4573j Spices: https://amzn.to/3Te95Xg Pans: https://amzn.to/3RQO44f Wooden utensils: https://amzn.to/3Lg0KhD Hand Blender/Chopper: https://amzn.to/4cOAFm5 Splatter Guard: https://amzn.to/3XMM7d7 Chopping Board: https://amzn.to/3zwNtyA Knives: https://amzn.to/4ctA1dv Mini Hand Food Processor: https://amzn.to/4bEidei Spice Grinder: https://amzn.to/4e59J1s ________________________________________ Ingredients • For the Marinade: o 1 kg bone-in lamb o 1 tbsp salt o 1 tbsp garam masala o 1 tsp crushed black peppercorns o 1 tsp turmeric o 1 tbsp chili powder o 2 tbsp Kashmiri red chili powder (for color and gentle heat) o 2 tbsp each of ginger & garlic paste o 1 cup desiccated coconut o 1 cup crushed fried onions o 3 tbsp yogurt (to tenderize the meat) o ¼ cup oil • For Cooking: o ¼ cup oil o 1 tbsp ghee o 2 cups water • Final Touches: o 3 tbsp tomato purée o 2 tbsp kasuri methi (crushed) o 3-4 large green chilies (whole or slit) o Generous handful of fresh coriander ________________________________________ Method 1. Marinate the Lamb: o In a large bowl, mix the lamb with salt, garam masala, black pepper, turmeric, chili powders, garlic-ginger paste, coconut, fried onions, yogurt, and oil. o Massage everything in well, ensuring the meat is fully coated in this fragrant spice mix. o If you can, let it rest in the fridge for at least an hour—it makes a world of difference! 2. Sear the Meat: o Heat ¼ cup oil and 1 tbsp ghee in a pan over medium heat. o Add the marinated lamb, making sure to scrape in every last bit of spice and yogurt. o Let it sear for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking. o The key here is patience—a beautiful crust means deep, rich flavours. 3. Build the Curry Base: o Once the lamb is nicely seared, pour in 2 cups of water from the side of the pan—gently, so you don’t wash away the spices. o Give it a stir, bring it to a gentle boil, then cover and simmer on low heat for 90 minutes. o No extra frying, no unnecessary steps—just let time do the work. 4. Halfway Check-In: o At the 45-minute mark, check on your curry. o The oil will have started to rise, the aroma will be out of this world, but the meat will need more time—so we let it continue simmering. 5. Final Transformation: o After 90 minutes, the lamb is melt-in-your-mouth tender, the gravy is thick and glossy, and the colour is deep and rich. o Stir in 3 tbsp tomato purée, cooking for a couple of minutes to add a natural sweetness and deepen the colour even more. 6. Finishing Touches: o Crush 2 tbsp kasuri methi into the curry for a beautiful earthy aroma. o Add whole or slit green chilies for a fresh kick. o Scatter over a generous handful of coriander—because no good curry is complete without it.
